back_translation: Then Moses and the elders of Israel commanded the people, saying: Keep all the commandment that I am commanding you today.
And it shall be, on the day you cross the Jordan to the land that the Lord your God is giving to you, you shall set up for yourself great stones and coat them with plaster.

And you shall write on them all the words of this law, when you cross over, so that you may enter the land that the Lord your God is giving to you, a land flowing with milk and honey, as the Lord God of your fathers has spoken to you.
And it shall be, when you cross the Jordan, you shall set up these stones that I am commanding you today on Mount Ebal, and you shall coat them with plaster.
And you shall build there an altar to the Lord your God, an altar of stones; you shall not wield iron upon them.
Whole stones you shall build the altar of the Lord your God, and you shall offer up burnt offerings on it to the Lord your God.
And you shall sacrifice peace offerings and eat there, and you shall rejoice before the Lord your God.
And you shall write on the stones all the words of this law, explaining clearly.
And Moses and the Levitical priests spoke to all Israel, saying: Be silent and listen, O Israel; this day you have become a people to the Lord your God.
And you shall listen to the voice of the Lord your God, and you shall do His commandments and His statutes that I am commanding you today.
And Moses commanded the people on that day, saying:
These shall stand to bless the people on Mount Gerizim when you cross the Jordan: Simeon and Levi and Judah, and Issachar and Joseph and Benjamin.
And these shall stand on the curse on Mount Ebal: Reuben, Gad and Asher, and Zebulun, Dan and Naphtali.
And the Levites shall answer and say to every man of Israel with a loud voice:
Cursed is the man who makes a carved image or molten image, an abomination of the Lord, the work of the hands of a craftsman, and sets it in secret. And all the people shall answer and say: Amen!
Cursed is the one who dishonors his father and his mother. And all the people shall say: Amen!
Cursed is the one who moves the boundary of his neighbor. And all the people shall say: Amen!
Cursed is the one who makes a blind man stray on the way. And all the people shall say: Amen!
Cursed is the one who turns aside the justice of a foreigner, an orphan, or a widow. And all the people shall say: Amen!
Cursed is the one who lies with the wife of his father, for he has uncovered the corner of his father. And all the people shall say: Amen!
Cursed is the one who lies with any animal. And all the people shall say: Amen!
Cursed is the one who lies with his sister, the daughter of his father or the daughter of his mother. And all the people shall say: Amen!
Cursed is the one who lies with his mother-in-law. And all the people shall say: Amen!
Cursed is the one who strikes his neighbor in secret. And all the people shall say: Amen!
Cursed is the one who takes a bribe to strike a life, innocent blood. And all the people shall say: Amen!
Cursed is the one who does not uphold the words of this law to do them. And all the people shall say: Amen!
